prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde (C₆H₅CHO) computed via DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation computed the neutral molecule, its cation (N−1, charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, multiplicity 2) states via SCF, and derived atomic Fukui descriptors (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity, f⁰ for radical character) and the dual descriptor from Mulliken partial charges. All three electronic states converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). Results show the aldehyde oxygen as the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.2384, atom 1) and the ortho carbon (atom 3) as the most electrophilic (f⁺ = 0.3994), consistent with the polarized aldehyde group and aromatic π-system. Full per-atom indices and dual descriptor values are reported above. Trustworthy: yes. No warnings or convergence issues.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
